      * #### clean

            [@Internal](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/annotation/Internal.html> "annotation in org.apache.flink.annotation")
            public <F> F clean(F f)

Returns a "closure-cleaned" version of the given function. Cleans only if closure cleaning is not disabled in the [`ExecutionConfig`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/ExecutionConfig.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common")

      * #### addOperator

            [@Internal](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/annotation/Internal.html> "annotation in org.apache.flink.annotation")
            public void addOperator([Transformation](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/dag/Transformation.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.dag")<?> transformation)

Adds an operator to the list of operators that should be executed when calling [`execute()`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html#execute-->).

When calling [`execute()`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html#execute-->) only the operators that where previously added to the list are executed.

This is not meant to be used by users. The API methods that create operators must call this method.

      * #### getConfiguration

            [@Internal](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/annotation/Internal.html> "annotation in org.apache.flink.annotation")
            public [ReadableConfig](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/configuration/ReadableConfig.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.configuration") getConfiguration()

Gives read-only access to the underlying configuration of this environment.

Note that the returned configuration might not be complete. It only contains options that have initialized the environment via [`StreamExecutionEnvironment(Configuration)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html#StreamExecutionEnvironment-org.apache.flink.configuration.Configuration->) or options that are not represented in dedicated configuration classes such as [`ExecutionConfig`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/ExecutionConfig.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common") or [`CheckpointConfig`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/CheckpointConfig.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment").

Use [`configure(ReadableConfig, ClassLoader)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html#configure-org.apache.flink.configuration.ReadableConfig-java.lang.ClassLoader->) to set options that are specific to this environment.

      * #### getExecutionEnvironment

            public static [StreamExecutionEnvironment](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment") getExecutionEnvironment()

Creates an execution environment that represents the context in which the program is currently executed. If the program is invoked standalone, this method returns a local execution environment, as returned by [`createLocalEnvironment()`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html#createLocalEnvironment-->).

Returns:
    The execution environment of the context in which the program is executed.
      * #### getExecutionEnvironment

            public static [StreamExecutionEnvironment](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment") getExecutionEnvironment([Configuration](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/configuration/Configuration.html> "class in org.apache.flink.configuration") configuration)

Creates an execution environment that represents the context in which the program is currently executed. If the program is invoked standalone, this method returns a local execution environment, as returned by [`createLocalEnvironment(Configuration)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html#createLocalEnvironment-org.apache.flink.configuration.Configuration->).

When executed from the command line the given configuration is stacked on top of the global configuration which comes from the `flink-conf.yaml`, potentially overriding duplicated options.

Parameters:
    `configuration` \- The configuration to instantiate the environment with.
Returns:
    The execution environment of the context in which the program is executed.
      * #### createLocalEnvironment

            public static [LocalStreamEnvironment](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/LocalStreamEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment") createLocalEnvironment()

Creates a [`LocalStreamEnvironment`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/LocalStreamEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment"). The local execution environment will run the program in a multi-threaded fashion in the same JVM as the environment was created in. The default parallelism of the local environment is the number of hardware contexts (CPU cores / threads), unless it was specified differently by [`setParallelism(int)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html#setParallelism-int->).

Returns:
    A local execution environment.
      * #### createLocalEnvironment

            public static [LocalStreamEnvironment](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/LocalStreamEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment") createLocalEnvironment(int parallelism)

Creates a [`LocalStreamEnvironment`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/LocalStreamEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment"). The local execution environment will run the program in a multi-threaded fashion in the same JVM as the environment was created in. It will use the parallelism specified in the parameter.

Parameters:
    `parallelism` \- The parallelism for the local environment.
Returns:
    A local execution environment with the specified parallelism.

      * #### createLocalEnvironmentWithWebUI

            [@PublicEvolving](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/annotation/PublicEvolving.html> "annotation in org.apache.flink.annotation")
            public static [StreamExecutionEnvironment](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment") createLocalEnvironmentWithWebUI([Configuration](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/configuration/Configuration.html> "class in org.apache.flink.configuration") conf)

Creates a [`LocalStreamEnvironment`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/LocalStreamEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment") for local program execution that also starts the web monitoring UI.

The local execution environment will run the program in a multi-threaded fashion in the same JVM as the environment was created in. It will use the parallelism specified in the parameter.

If the configuration key 'rest.port' was set in the configuration, that particular port will be used for the web UI. Otherwise, the default port (8081) will be used.

      * #### createRemoteEnvironment

            public static [StreamExecutionEnvironment](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment") createRemoteEnvironment([String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") host,
                                                                             int port,
                                                                             [String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")... jarFiles)

Creates a [`RemoteStreamEnvironment`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/RemoteStreamEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment"). The remote environment sends (parts of) the program to a cluster for execution. Note that all file paths used in the program must be accessible from the cluster. The execution will use no parallelism, unless the parallelism is set explicitly via [`setParallelism(int)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html#setParallelism-int->).

Parameters:
    `host` \- The host name or address of the master (JobManager), where the program should be executed.
    `port` \- The port of the master (JobManager), where the program should be executed.
    `jarFiles` \- The JAR files with code that needs to be shipped to the cluster. If the program uses user-defined functions, user-defined input formats, or any libraries, those must be provided in the JAR files.
Returns:
    A remote environment that executes the program on a cluster.
      * #### createRemoteEnvironment

            public static [StreamExecutionEnvironment](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment") createRemoteEnvironment([String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") host,
                                                                             int port,
                                                                             int parallelism,
                                                                             [String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")... jarFiles)

Creates a [`RemoteStreamEnvironment`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/RemoteStreamEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment"). The remote environment sends (parts of) the program to a cluster for execution. Note that all file paths used in the program must be accessible from the cluster. The execution will use the specified parallelism.

Parameters:
    `host` \- The host name or address of the master (JobManager), where the program should be executed.
    `port` \- The port of the master (JobManager), where the program should be executed.
    `parallelism` \- The parallelism to use during the execution.
    `jarFiles` \- The JAR files with code that needs to be shipped to the cluster. If the program uses user-defined functions, user-defined input formats, or any libraries, those must be provided in the JAR files.
Returns:
    A remote environment that executes the program on a cluster.
      * #### createRemoteEnvironment

            public static [StreamExecutionEnvironment](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/StreamExecutionEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment") createRemoteEnvironment([String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") host,
                                                                             int port,
                                                                             [Configuration](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/configuration/Configuration.html> "class in org.apache.flink.configuration") clientConfig,
                                                                             [String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")... jarFiles)

Creates a [`RemoteStreamEnvironment`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/environment/RemoteStreamEnvironment.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.environment"). The remote environment sends (parts of) the program to a cluster for execution. Note that all file paths used in the program must be accessible from the cluster. The execution will use the specified parallelism.

Parameters:
    `host` \- The host name or address of the master (JobManager), where the program should be executed.
    `port` \- The port of the master (JobManager), where the program should be executed.
    `clientConfig` \- The configuration used by the client that connects to the remote cluster.
    `jarFiles` \- The JAR files with code that needs to be shipped to the cluster. If the program uses user-defined functions, user-defined input formats, or any libraries, those must be provided in the JAR files.
Returns:
    A remote environment that executes the program on a cluster.

      * #### registerCachedFile

            public void registerCachedFile([String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") filePath,
                                           [String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") name)

Registers a file at the distributed cache under the given name. The file will be accessible from any user-defined function in the (distributed) runtime under a local path. Files may be local files (which will be distributed via BlobServer), or files in a distributed file system. The runtime will copy the files temporarily to a local cache, if needed.

The [`RuntimeContext`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RuntimeContext.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions") can be obtained inside UDFs via [`RichFunction.getRuntimeContext()`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFunction.html#getRuntimeContext-->) and provides access [`DistributedCache`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/cache/DistributedCache.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.cache") via [`RuntimeContext.getDistributedCache()`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RuntimeContext.html#getDistributedCache-->).

Parameters:
    `filePath` \- The path of the file, as a URI (e.g. "file:///some/path" or "hdfs://host:port/and/path")
    `name` \- The name under which the file is registered.
      * #### registerCachedFile

            public void registerCachedFile([String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") filePath,
                                           [String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") name,
                                           boolean executable)

Registers a file at the distributed cache under the given name. The file will be accessible from any user-defined function in the (distributed) runtime under a local path. Files may be local files (which will be distributed via BlobServer), or files in a distributed file system. The runtime will copy the files temporarily to a local cache, if needed.

The [`RuntimeContext`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RuntimeContext.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions") can be obtained inside UDFs via [`RichFunction.getRuntimeContext()`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFunction.html#getRuntimeContext-->) and provides access [`DistributedCache`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/cache/DistributedCache.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.cache") via [`RuntimeContext.getDistributedCache()`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RuntimeContext.html#getDistributedCache-->).

Parameters:
    `filePath` \- The path of the file, as a URI (e.g. "file:///some/path" or "hdfs://host:port/and/path")
    `name` \- The name under which the file is registered.
    `executable` \- flag indicating whether the file should be executable
